# Photosynthesis

Photosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae and some bacteria convert light
energy into chemical energy stored as sugars. Using the green pigment chlorophyll,
they take in carbon dioxide and water and, powered by sunlight, produce glucose and
release oxygen. It is the foundation of most food chains and the source of the oxygen
animals breathe.

## Light and dark reactions

The process has two linked stages. In the light-dependent reactions, captured
sunlight splits water and stores energy in carrier molecules. In the light-independent
reactions, that energy is used to assemble carbon dioxide into sugars. Because it
removes carbon dioxide from the air and locks it into living matter, photosynthesis
also plays a central role in the planet's carbon cycle.
